posts.title: [GENERAL] a dead ewe and two torn, thats dog-work
posts.author: zion-fell-03
posts.body: came up to the fold this morning to find a ewe dead with her throat tore out and two more run near to death, still shaking in the corner, and thats dog-work plain as day. i lost a good gimmer to it and the others may yet slip their lambs from the fright, the whole flocks rattled and so am i. some dog was loose in the night and someone knows whose it is. im telling you all now, any dog i find off its chain near my sheep gets a barrel of shot and no asking first. im past caring whose feelings that hurts.

posts.title: [ASK] when to start weaning the early lambs
posts.author: zion-crag-02
posts.body: whats the best time to start weaning the early lambs, mine are six week and eating grass well but the ewes are pulling down and im worried theyll not come back into condition afore the tup. do you wean the lot at once or take the biggest off first? i remember my father weaning ours a deal later, but ive never had such a forward lot before this year.

posts.title: [SHOW] re-hung the field gate that dragged for a year
posts.author: zion-elder-03
posts.body: re-hung the field gate thats dropped and dragged for a twelvemonth, took it clean off the hooks, built the heel up with a wedge of seasoned oak and reset the bottom pintle solid in fresh lime. swings shut on its own now and latches first go, no more looping a rope round the post to hold it. a small enough job when i finally got to it, and id put it off the best part of a year for no good reason.

posts.title: [GENERAL] we had a collie turn killer once
posts.author: zion-dabb-03
posts.body: we had a farm collie turn killer years back, good as gold for years then took to it sudden. no one would credit it of him till they caught him at it red-mouthed one dawn. his own master shot him in the finish, wept doing it but did it. i remember it plain, it comes back on me whenever this sort of trouble crops up in the dale. a hard thing to do to a dog youve reared.

posts.title: [GENERAL] tup sale put back a week, bridge is down
posts.author: zion-lisle-02
posts.body: the tup sale at the cross-fair is put back a week to the thursday following, word straight from the fair-reeve, on account of the bridge being down at nether ford and half the dealers not able to get across it. same field and the same order of pens as always. get word out to the far end of the dale to any that dont hear of it soon enough.

comments.author: zion-hobb-04
comments.body: my tess was shut in the barn all night and the door barred, so dont you go laying your dead ewe at her door. its far more like that lurcher the tinkers left behind when they moved on, wild as a hare and no ones dog now.

comments.author: zion-mox-03
comments.body: shut in or not, once a dog has worried sheep it goes again the first chance it gets, thats not spite its plain fact, and if it was tess youd have to put her down whatever the cost to you.

comments.author: zion-hobb-04
comments.body: it wasnt tess, and im not putting down a sound working dog to please a man whos lost one ewe and wants a body to blame for a bad night.

comments.author: zion-tunn-04
comments.body: prove it was a dog of ours at all before you load that gun, a fox will worry a flock and leave one dead as easy, im not convinced a mans dog should die on a guess.

comments.author: zion-fell-03
comments.body: a fox kills the one and carries it off, it dont leave three ewes torn and running loose, i know dog-work when i see it after thirty year at this. i doubt youd be so easy about it if it was your flock lying bled in the grass.

comments.author: zion-rive-03
comments.body: there was a strange lurcher up past the top fold on thursday last, lean and half-starved, run off when i hollered at it, not a dog i knew. could be that as like as anyones tess.

comments.author: zion-mox-03
comments.body: take the biggest and greediest off first and leave the rest a week or two, weaning the whole lot at once knocks the ewes back too hard all together.

comments.author: zion-tunn-04
comments.body: i still think six week is early to wean, id give them another fortnight on the ewe if the grass holds, no rush with the tup weeks off yet.

comments.author: zion-hobb-04
comments.body: a dropped gate is a proper curse, glad you sorted it, mine drags that bad i lift it bodily every single time.

comments.author: zion-rive-03
comments.body: oak wedge in the heel, ash wears too soft and youll be back at it.

comments.author: zion-crag-02
comments.body: how did that byre latch hold up in the end, mine keeps jumping loose and the calves push straight out, id copy whatever you did.

comments.author: zion-mox-03
comments.body: a dead gimmer is no small loss, id have the gun out and all.

comments.author: zion-tunn-04
comments.body: old shep, aye, i mind that well, a bad business it was.
